classes.contract: describes the legal input or wire vocabulary; owned by the schema/protocol source
classes.policy: a bounded run-quality, cost, or polling default with an ordinary override surface
classes.closed: a substrate safety, spec, or backend-equivalence invariant; not an ordinary DX setting

limits.value: string semantic keys are closed to pattern, minLength, and maxLength
limits.reason: Tool argument legality is a schema contract and must be projected and locally validated from one dialect source.

limits.value: workspace paths reject empty, NUL, parent escape, and host path semantics
limits.reason: Path acceptance is part of the workspace tool argument contract, not a consumer adapter policy.
Turn count changes run cost and quality for one submit; it does not change substrate safety.

limits.value: default 2 correction retries and 2 jittered exponential execution retries
limits.reason: Tool retry correction and execution backoff are per-run recovery policy.
Submit time is a per-run budget and is already represented in the submit DTO.
Token budget is a run policy, not a schema or safety invariant.
Projection wait bounds read latency for one caller; callers can pick their own patience without changing projection truth.
Drain iteration count is an operational work budget for one pump invocation.
Trigger acquire deadline is declared by the trigger implementation that owns the external step.
Workspace file preview/write size is an integration policy for one tool bundle.
Command length is a workspace tool policy, not a substrate-wide safety proof.
Workspace command timeout is selected by the workspace host/tool bundle.
Output projection size is a tool bundle policy and remains separate from actuator correctness.

limits.value: default 100 matches and 4096 bytes per match
limits.overrideSurface: glob/grep tool args maxMatches and maxBytesPerMatch
limits.reason: Search result volume is caller-selected output shaping.
Heartbeat cadence is a transport liveness policy for one stream consumer.

limits.value: provider call timeout defaults to 60000ms when submit time budget is absent
limits.reason: The provider timeout currently has no manifest/submit policy source; opening it needs a declared policy axis rather than changing a hot-path constant.

limits.value: 8 attempts, 1000ms initial delay, 60000ms max delay, multiplier 2
limits.reason: Dispatch retry shape is backend-neutral protocol semantics and must stay identical across production backends until a protocol policy axis exists.

limits.value: event reads are capped at 1000 rows per page
limits.reason: Page caps are backend equivalence and storage protection constraints; callers may request smaller pages but not redefine the cap per submit.

limits.value: projection list reads are capped at 1000 rows per page
limits.reason: Projection page caps must remain backend-consistent until a protocol-level page policy exists.

limits.value: W3C traceparent regex; tracestate max 512 chars and 32 members
limits.reason: Trace context shape is external protocol compliance and must not be relaxed as a DX setting.
